PipChat — CSAE Safety Standards

For the people, By the people
Zero‑tolerance for CSAE/CSAM

PipChat is committed to protecting minors and preventing child sexual abuse and exploitation (CSAE/CSAM). We maintain a strict zero‑tolerance policy and enforce robust safety standards across user‑generated content, profiles, media uploads, and messaging. These standards apply to all users, communities, and content on PipChat.

Any CSAE/CSAM content is strictly prohibited and results in immediate enforcement.

Last updated: January 24, 2026

1) Zero‑Tolerance Policy

We do not allow:

  • Any content depicting, promoting, or facilitating sexual abuse or exploitation of minors.
  • Requests, threats, grooming behavior, or attempts to obtain sexual content involving minors.
  • Sharing, soliciting, or distributing CSAM/CSAE materials, links, or code words.
  • Any sexualized depiction of minors, real or fictional.

Violations result in immediate content removal, account termination, and reporting as required by law.

2) Prohibited Content and Behaviors

  • CSAM/CSAE imagery or video, including generated or manipulated content.
  • Sexual conversations with, about, or targeting minors.
  • Grooming, coercion, exploitation, or trafficking‑related content involving minors.
  • Any attempt to circumvent safety systems or moderation.

3) Detection and Moderation

PipChat uses a combination of automated and human review to detect and prevent CSAE:

  • Media safety classifiers for images and video (including pre‑upload checks).
  • Keyword and pattern matching to detect high‑risk terms and grooming behavior.
  • Manual review queues for flagged or suspicious content.
  • Age‑based safeguards and parental controls for minors.

Content that appears unsafe is blocked or placed under review before it is visible to others.

4) Reporting and User Safety

Users can report content directly in the app using the flag/report options. We review reports promptly.

  • In‑app reporting: available on posts, profiles, replies, chats, and catalog items.
  • Email reporting: support@pipchat.in

If there is an imminent threat or suspected abuse, contact local emergency services.

5) Enforcement Actions

When CSAE/CSAM is detected or confirmed, PipChat may:

  • Immediately remove the content.
  • Suspend or permanently terminate the account.
  • Preserve evidence for legal compliance.
  • Report to relevant authorities, including NCMEC (U.S.) or other regional agencies as required by law.

6) Protection for Minors

  • Minimum age requirement (no accounts for users under 13).
  • Parental approval workflows for minors.
  • Restricted access for age‑sensitive features and media.
  • Enhanced moderation for accounts identified as minors.

7) Data Handling and Retention

For safety and legal obligations, PipChat may retain relevant data and logs associated with CSAE investigations for the minimum period required by law. Access to such data is restricted to authorized personnel only.

8) Cooperation With Authorities

PipChat cooperates with law enforcement and child safety organizations when required. We respond to valid legal requests and preserve evidence in compliance with applicable laws.

9) Community Education

We provide clear safety guidance and encourage users to report suspicious behavior. PipChat aims to maintain a safe, civic‑minded environment inspired by community discussions under the Pipal tree tradition.

10) Contact

For CSAE/CSAM concerns, please contact:

Email: support@pipchat.in
